name: Darshan I/O Profile Analysis
summary: Analyze Darshan logs for HPC I/O behavior and bottleneck evidence.
description: This skill helps users and support teams turn existing Darshan logs into reviewable I/O evidence, including parser summaries, optional PDF commands, DXT trace notes, and a checklist for interpreting POSIX, MPI-IO, HDF5, and PnetCDF access patterns.

tools.name: darshan-parser
tools.notes: Primary darshan-util command for text-format log analysis.

tools.name: darshan-config
tools.notes: Records Darshan utility configuration and default log path when available.

tools.name: darshan-job-summary.pl
tools.notes: Optional PDF summary generator; disabled by default in the example script.

tools.name: darshan-summary-per-file.sh
tools.notes: Optional per-file PDF generator; disabled by default because it can create many files.

tools.name: darshan-dxt-parser
tools.notes: Optional parser for logs collected with DXT instrumentation enabled.

inputs.name: darshan_log
inputs.description: Existing Darshan log file produced by a previously instrumented workload.

inputs.name: output_dir
inputs.description: Local directory where parser output, optional command notes, and review notes should be written.

inputs.name: job_context
inputs.description: Optional public-safe context such as application name, filesystem, scheduler job id, and expected I/O pattern.

outputs.name: darshan_parser_report
outputs.description: Text output from darshan-parser, plus optional perf, file, and total summaries when the utility supports them.

outputs.name: optional_summary_commands
outputs.description: Reviewable commands for PDF, per-file, and DXT summaries that remain disabled unless explicitly requested.

outputs.name: review_notes
outputs.description: Checklist for interpreting I/O modules, small I/O, shared-file access, imbalance, metadata pressure, and privacy-sensitive log fields.

examples.title: Review instrumentation choices before collecting new logs

tests.description: Validate the manifest and referenced examples.
Check shell syntax without parsing a real Darshan log.
Run the script against a public-safe Darshan log and confirm parser output, optional command notes, and review notes are written to the output directory.
